zh.lifeSummary: Adam Prescott de Parr於從大約1185年到1245年出生。他至少有1個兒子。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: Adam Prescott de Parr于从大约1185年到1245年出生。他至少有1个儿子。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.
Indian: variant of   Dey  .

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Parr
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: habitational name from Parr in Lancashire, or from residence at an enclosure (Middle English parr, Old English pearr).
German: from Middle Low German parre ‘parish, district, minister's house’; a metonymic occupational name for a parson or for someone who worked in a parsonage or manse. Compare Pfarr .
English: variant of Pear with the common late Middle English and early modern English change of -er- to -ar-.

variants.name: Pare
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Andre, Henri, Laurent, Normand, Fernand, Alphonse, Clermont, Elphege, Alain, Benoit, Gaston, Girard.
French (Paré): occupational name for someone who finished cloth, from the past participle of parer ‘to prepare’.
English: variant of   Parr  .

variants.name: Parman
meanings: Americanized form of German Parmann: variant of Parr .
Possibly an altered form of German Bärmann (see Barmann ).
English: variant of   Pearman  .

variants.name: Porr
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Ulrike, Gunther.
German:
metonymic occupational name for a grower or seller of leeks or other vegetables, from Middle High German porre, pforre ‘leek’ (from Latin porrum).

variants.name: Pfarr
meanings: South German: from Middle High German pfarr ‘district, parish’ or pfarre(r) ‘parish priest’, hence an occupational name for a parson, or a topographic name for someone who lived near the parsonage.

nameMeanings.name: Adam
meanings: Hebrew: The Scripture name, in Hebrew, Adham, signifying 'man;' literally earthy, red earth; Rudolph, however, says the name in Ethiopic means ' to be fair, beautiful.' Hence the nurse-name Addy, little Adam.

nameMeanings.name: Prescott
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: habitational name from any of the places so called, in southwestern Lancashire (now Merseyside), Gloucestershire, Oxfordshire, Shropshire, and Devon, all of which are named from Old English prēost ‘priest’ + cot ‘cottage’, ‘dwelling’. The surname is most common in Lancashire, and so it seems likely that the first of these places is the most frequent source. It is also present in Ireland, being recorded there first in the 15th century.

History: John Prescott of Standish, Lancaster, England, arrived in NewEngland in 1640 and in 1643 was one of the first settlers ofLancaster, MA. His descendants include several prominent Americans ofthe revolutionary war, including Samuel Prescott, born in Concord, MA,in 1751, whose fame lies in completing the midnight ride of warning in1775 after Paul Revere was captured.

variants.name: Prescod
meanings: English (Durham): variant of   Prescott  .
